support: Central randomized multicenter design; baseline groups broadly comparable.
Functional unblinding was likely and not formally assessed; this matters less for objective SAEs but can affect reporting and rescue decisions.
Safety population and follow-up were substantially complete; denominators are stated.
Investigator attribution of relatedness is subjective and severe-event categories do not fully map to the prespecified estimand.
Safety is reported through day 9/day 43 rather than exact 24-hour and 7-day windows; rescue/observation counts are incomplete.
Strong direct trial, but functional unblinding and incompatible safety windows prevent a low-risk judgment for this exact result.
Randomized double-blind active-placebo design; allocation methods require duplicate full protocol review.
Allocation guesses were correct in nearly all participants, showing major functional unblinding.
Short-term follow-up appears complete; long-term attrition is less relevant to the 7-day estimand.
Some tables report event counts rather than participants; severe anxiety onset and exact window are not provided.
Safety summaries use dosing/day15/day42/day365 checkpoints rather than the exact estimand windows.
Direct and informative but small, functionally unblinded, and not reported in estimand-compatible participant-level windows.
